section.heading: Study of barriers to participation of women in apprenticeable occupations and nontraditional occupations

subsection.heading: Study
subsection.chapeau: With funds available to the Secretary to carry out the operations of the Department of Labor in fiscal years 1994 and 1995, the Secretary shall conduct a study of the participation of women in apprenticeable occupations and nontraditional occupations. The study shall examine—

paragraph.content: the barriers to participation of women in apprenticeable occupations and nontraditional occupations;
the retention rates for women in apprenticeable occupations and nontraditional occupations;
strategies for retaining women in apprenticeable occupations and nontraditional occupations;
the effectiveness of the technical assistance provided by the community-based organizations; and
other relevant issues affecting the participation of women in apprenticeable occupations and nontraditional occupations.

content.p: Not later than 2 years after October 27, 1992, the Secretary shall submit to the Congress a report containing a summary of the results of the study described in subsection (a) and such recommendations as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.
